name: Vishnu Wood
abstract: William Clifford "Vishnu" Wood (born November 7, 1937), also known as Vishnu Bill Wood, is an American double bass player and educator. Born in Wilkesboro, North Carolina, he moved to Detroit when he was ten, where he started playing trumpet and later taking up double bass.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Randy Weston Sextet
Newport Jazz Festival: The In Crowd

works.t: Sometimes I Feel Like a Motherless Child
Were You There (When They Crucified My Lord)
Joshua Fit de Battle of Jericho

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: And they turned it into an emergency fund because Vishnu Wood and Jamil Nasser, and Jimmy Owens came to them and said, “There’s all these musicians having terrible issues. We need to do something.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 22, 2026

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: There was a bass player I had met in Detroit, named Vishnu Wood... I met him in Detroit. It turns out he had a place next door to Bill. I wound up staying with him and sharing the rent.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: There was Vishnu Wood, Bill Wood on bass -- two basses, Eddie Blackwell and the three horns. I played -- just had one little concert
